source_jobs.title: Founding Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Dataleap

We raised $3M from YC and angels like Aravind (CEO Perplexity) and Rahul Vohra (Superhuman) to build the agentic operating system for the enterprise. We have thousands of agents deployed across scale-up startups and Fortune 500 companies. We have PMF and are doubling revenue every month. And we have far more enterprise inbound than we can handle.

About the role

We need someone who operates at CTO-level. Someone who can take a vague problem, scope it, build it end-to-end, and ship something that actually provides value. Backend-heavy, AI-native, able to go full stack when needed. No hand-holding. No waiting for specs. You're a full-stack engineer, an AI engineer, and a product engineer in one person. Former founder is a strong plus.

What you'll do

 - Own large parts of the product end-to-end, from user insight to production.

 - Build and improve our agentic systems. Make them more accurate, more reliable, more powerful.

 - Work at the frontier of AI engineering: context engineering, tool use, agent architectures.

 - Talk to users. Understand their pain. Scope features that actually solve problems.

 - Push AI-first development — Claude Code, coding agents, automation. You should be obsessed with scaling yourself.

 - Review code fast and maintain a high bar across the codebase.

Our stack: Go backend, Vite/TypeScript frontend.

You're a fit if

 - You're a razor-sharp engineer who solves hard problems fast with the code taste to review PRs at speed without dropping the bar.

 - You have deep product sense. You build for users, not for code's sake.

 - You love Go and care about clean, maintainable code. Backend-heavy, but not allergic to frontend.

 - You've built AI products, ideally agentic systems or AI coding tools.

 - You've built something from scratch: a startup, a side project, a feature that shipped. You thrive in ambiguity and don't need a PM to tell you what to build.

AI research background or experience at an AI coding startup is a plus. But what matters most is that you're exceptional and hungry.

What you get

 - Work alongside a brilliant design engineer who makes everything look and feel incredible.

 - A CTO and cofounder who will push you harder than anyone has before and make you grow faster than you thought possible.

 - Unlimited AI budget. We practice what we preach.

 - Generous equity and comp reflecting the outsized impact this role will have.

 - A seat at the table. This is a founding role, you'll shape the product and the company.

We're building the platform that redefines how people work. If you want to build at the frontier, reach out.

source_jobs.title: Working Student Integrations
source_jobs.description:
About Dataleap

Dataleap (YC S24) is building the agentic operating system for the enterprise. Supported by $3M in funding from YC and industry-leading angels (including Aravind from Perplexity and Rahul Vohra from Superhuman), we’re scaling rapidly with thousands of agents deployed, doubling revenue every month, and facing more enterprise demand than we can handle.

About the Role

We're looking for a working student (20h/week, €30/hour) to co-own our integrations—this is real ownership from the start. You’ll work directly with the founders, ship code to production in your first week, and have an immediate impact on our agentic AI platform.

If you’d like to be part of a fast-growing YC startup, develop deep technical expertise, and potentially join full-time after graduation, we want to meet you.

What You’ll Do

 - Own, design, and implement integrations for our agentic systems end to end.

 - Work directly with the founders and be a key contributor from day one.

 - Ship working software to production quickly—no red tape.

 - Build and improve our tooling and workflows (APIs, automation, AI-first processes).

 - Interface with customers and our core team to understand and solve real-world integration challenges.

 - Shape the technical future of our integrations foundation.

You’re a Fit If

 - You have strong technical skills, especially in Python and TypeScript.

 - You’re comfortable working with APIs and integrating systems.

 - You thrive in fast-paced, ambiguous environments and like to take ownership.

 - You care about code quality and delivering value.

 - You’re eager to learn, build, and grow with us—ideally interested in a full-time role after graduation.

What We Offer

 - Competitive student compensation (€30/hour for 20 hours/week).

 - Direct mentorship and collaboration with experienced founders and top engineers.

 - Real responsibility and the chance to see your work in production from week one.

 - The opportunity to transition to a full-time role after graduation.

 - Be part of a high-growth YC startup shaping the future of enterprise AI.

source_jobs.title: Founding Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE)
source_jobs.description:
About Dataleap

We raised $3M from YC and angels like Aravind (CEO Perplexity) and Rahul Vohra (Superhuman) to build the agentic operating system for the enterprise. Thousands of agents deployed, doubling revenue every month. Far more enterprise inbound than we can handle.

About the role

You are the engineer on the ground at our biggest DACH customers, based in Munich, working hand in hand with our Deployment Strategist.

When a multibillion-dollar company decides to go AI-native with Dataleap, you show up at their office, sit with their teams, find the workflows that matter, and ship agents that run in production. You scope, build, integrate, debug, and teach. You leave behind systems the customer can own. Then you do it again at the next account.

Part forward-deployed engineer, part founding engineer, part technical sparring partner for the customer's CTO. End-to-end. No PM. No spec. You walk into ambiguity and ship.

You also build on our core product. When you hit a platform limitation at a customer, you don't file a ticket, you work directly with engineering to close the gap, most often by writing the fix yourself.

What you'll do

 - Embed with our largest DACH customers. Run workshops, hackathons, and implementation sprints on site.

 - Ship the highest-leverage use cases end to end: complex agents, custom integrations, MCP servers, multi-step workflows wired into the systems they already run on (SAP, internal APIs, legacy tooling).

 - Sit with customer CTOs and platform teams as a technical peer.

 - Push our own product forward. Identify what's blocking customer value and work with engineering to fix it.

 - Build agents to automate your own work. Eliminate manual work from your role.

You're a fit if

 - You're a strong full-stack engineer who can also go deep. Bonus if you know Go.

 - You're an AI engineer in practice. You've shipped agentic systems, tool-use loops, and LLM features that work under load.

 - You live in Claude Code, Cursor, and coding agents. You've already scaled yourself in ways most engineers haven't.

 - You can scope a complex use case in a customer meeting at 11am and have it working by EOD.

 - You've shipped something from scratch. Founder or early-employee experience is a strong plus.

 - You communicate sharply with technical and non-technical stakeholders alike.

 - You're high agency, high clock speed, low ego, high ownership — and you want to work very hard and be deeply obsessed.

 - You speak German at a native level and are based in Munich or ready to move.

We index on energy, values, and slope — not years of experience.

What you get

 - Direct exposure to my network at YC, Founders Inc, and CDTM.

 - The fastest growth opportunity of your career. I'll make sure of it personally.

 - Unlimited AI budget. We practice what we preach.

 - Top-of-market equity for a founding hire — see the range above.

 - A seat at the table as we build out the European org from Munich.

 - If you ever want to start your own company, I'll back you.

If this resonates, reach out.

source_jobs.title: Founding Deployment Strategist
source_jobs.description:
About Dataleap

We raised $3M from YC and angels like Aravind (CEO Perplexity) and Rahul Vohra (Superhuman) to build the agentic operating system for the enterprise. We have thousands of agents deployed across scale-up startups and Fortune 500 companies. We have PMF and are doubling revenue every month. And we have far more enterprise inbound than we can handle.


About the role

This role is what I used to do, and I need someone to do it better than I did.
You will lead the rollout of Dataleap across the European market, based in Munich, and will work directly with me — the CEO and founder.


From day 1, you are not an account manager or a vendor, but a trusted partner who helps our customers AI-transform their entire org. Each customer is like a mini consulting project where it's your job to diagnose where AI creates the most leverage, identify the highest-value use cases, train the org, build the agents alongside them, and turn the company into an AI-native operator. You own the outcome.


As we scale, you help build the team underneath you by hiring, owning, and shaping Dataleap in Europe. Basically a founder in a founder-led company.


This role is a front row seat for AI - you learn how to deploy AI in big corporations and play at the forefront of development. You can sit with a CTO and credibly debate model selection, eval strategy, data residency, and integration architecture, because you've shipped it yourself.


What you'll do

 - Find the use cases that matter. Build the first agents yourself to prove the value, then scale adoption org-wide.

 - Host agent hackathons, discuss rollout strategy with the C-Suite, and help them make their companies AI-native.

 - Navigate complex political landscapes inside huge organizations. Find the champions. Disarm the blockers. Build relationships at every level, from end users to the CEO.

 - Be the voice of the customer to engineering and product. Push hard for what they need. Push back when they're wrong.

 - Build agents and workflows in Dataleap to automate your own role. We expect you to systematically eliminate manual work from your job, not use AI as a productivity buff.

 - Lead the European rollout from Munich. Build the team underneath you as we scale.

 - Find the thousand things I haven't listed here, and just do them.
   

You're a fit if

 - You're an AI power user. Not "exploring AI" — you live in it. You're shipping agents on weekends and have opinions on model routing, eval design, and where current systems break.

 - You're high agency and high clock speed: you drive the agenda, prioritize ruthlessly, and communicate sharp and to the point.

 - You're low ego, high ownership. There's unsexy work and you'll do it without flinching. You want to work hard and be deeply obsessed.

 - You're commercially sharp — renewals, expansion, and multi-stakeholder navigation are second nature.

 - You're comfortable being the youngest in a room of executives and still the one in charge.

 - You speak German at a native level and are based in Munich (or ready to move here).

I'll index heavily on energy, values, and slope.


What you get

 - Direct exposure to the best people at YC, Founders Inc, and CDTM.

 - Frequent trips to San Francisco, catapulting you to the absolute forefront of agentic AI.

 - The best mentors and coaches for whatever problem you're working on.

 - Unlimited AI budget to automate your own work.

 - Generous equity and comp that reflects the impact I expect this role to have.

 - The path to build and lead a team as we scale across Europe.

 - I will personally make sure this is the fastest growth opportunity of your career.

 - If you ever want to start your own company, I'll back you.

notes: YC-backed startup with rapid growth and high demand for enterprise solutions
